data.title: For some small businesses, a tariff refund isn't worth the pain of pursuing it
Rockwell Automation Announces Ronal Group Will Modernize Remote Access with New, Centralized System
data.description:
Rockwell Automation, Inc. announced that Ronal Group, a leading maker of wheels for consumer and commercial vehicles, is consolidating remote access across sites worldwide using Rockwell Automation secure remote access technology. As part of its ongoing commitment to operational excellence and regulatory alignment, the RONAL GROUP has adopted a centralized approach to managing authorized external access. The solution supports encrypted communication, role-based authorization and comprehensive monitoring capabilities in line with applicable regulatory requirements, including NIS2.

The implementation enables the RONAL GROUP to manage approved remote access in a consistent and standardized manner across its operations. Access rights are assigned based on clearly defined roles and operational needs and are subject to defined governance processes.

data.title: Trump to meet tech giants on energy pledge ahead of midterms
Cortechs.Ai Announces A Strategic Partnership with Siemens Healthineers
data.description:
Cortechs.ai announced a strategic partnership with Siemens Healthineers to integrate and distribute Cortechs.ai's FDA-cleared NeuroQuant Lesion Surveillance through the Siemens Healthineers Digital Marketplace. The expanded portfolio includes the latest FDA-cleared NeuroQuant Lesion Surveillance module, which uniquely provides automated segmentation and volumetric quantification, along with longitudinal tracking of lesion size and dynamics over time. These capabilities provide clinicians with important quantitative insights to support assessment across a broad range of neurological conditions, including the early detection and monitoring of ARIA in Alzheimer's patients receiving anti-amyloid therapies.

This partnership will deliver seamless integration of NeuroQuant Lesion Surveillance into the imaging ecosystem of Siemens Healthineers, enabling radiologists and neurologists to access quantitative treatment monitoring data directly within their existing workflows. By integrating NeuroQuant's proven capabilities for supporting ARIA surveillance into the Siemens Healthineers ecosystem, healthcare providers are empowered to deliver more precise and personalized care for patients undergoing Alzheimer's disease treatment.

ASM International reaches new historic highs
data.description:
Expected Q1 2026 revenue would therefore exceed the consensus of €764m.

The Dutch semiconductor equipment specialist highlighted the resilience of its Advanced Logic (advanced logic: processors, AI chips, high-performance computing) and Foundry (foundry: plants that manufacture chips on behalf of customers that do not have fabs) segments, supported by the rise of artificial intelligence. Initial spending related to 1.4-nanometre pilot lines is expected in H2 2026.

In addition, ASM International is forecasting healthy growth in DRAM memory sales and an increase in revenue in China, revising upward its guidance that had initially pointed to a double-digit decline.

ASM says it is ready to support its customers' industrial ramp-up: "by supporting their innovations, we are meeting the sustained growth in demand for AI computing infrastructure".

For fiscal year 2025, its gross margin reached a record high of 51.8% thanks to a very favourable product mix, versus 50.5% in 2024.

Operating profit rose to €956.9m, from €821m a year ago. The operating margin for full-year 2025 increased to a record level of 30.2%.

Net profit increased 5.54% y-o-y to €723.7m.

"2025 was another year of progress for ASM. Revenue rose 12% at constant exchange rates, marking our ninth consecutive year of double-digit growth," said Hichem M'Saad, ASM's CEO. It reached a record €3.2bn in 2025, driven mainly by the advanced logic and foundry segment, as customers stepped up investment in 2nm GAA production capabilities.

Order momentum in the fourth quarter of 2025

"Fourth-quarter revenue, at €698m, is down 7% y-o-y at constant exchange rates, but exceeds our forecast of €630m to €660m, as we announced earlier in January. Order intake for the quarter reached €803m, up 26% versus the previous quarter at constant exchange rates. This result is also above our initial indications, supported by stronger demand in leading-edge logic and foundry, as well as a rebound in orders from our Chinese customers," it stressed.

Commenting on the performance, ING said that "ASM had already announced in January order intake well above expectations (beating the consensus at the time by 20%)". "Today's full release brings additional positives: fourth-quarter 2025 gross margin and operating expenses (OPEX) are both better than expected. This translates into normalised net profit 10% above the consensus (compiled by the company and updated after the pre-announcement).

The Dutch bank is expecting "an upward revision to the consensus for 2026 annual revenue of around 1% to 5% and EBIT growth of 5% to 10%.

Meanwhile, Oddo BHF forecasts 2026 revenue up 14% to €3.6bn (versus a consensus of €3.5bn) and up 18% for 2027, to €4.27bn, versus a consensus of €4.23bn). Maintaining its outperform rating with a higher target price (from €820 to €880), the broker raises its gross margin assumption to 50.2%, from 49.8%, in light of its comments on a recovery in activity in China where margins are more favourable.

For the analyst, ASM is ideally positioned to meet its 2027 targets: expected revenue between €3.7bn and €4.6bn, gross margin expected between 47% and 51%, and an operating margin between 28% and 32%.

Following this annual performance, ASM International will propose a dividend of €3.25 per share for 2025 and will launch a new share buyback program of €150m.

IAEA: Near Isfahan nuclear site, damage is visible at two buildings
Adidas store in Israel hit by bomb, says chief commercial officer
data.description:
BERLIN, March 4 (Reuters) - Adidas' chief commercial officer said on Wednesday that one of the company's franchise stores in Israel had been hit by a bomb, but no one had been injured, amid the unfolding war in Iran.

"We had one franchise store in Israel which was hit indeed by an attack three days ago," Adidas' chief commercial officer Mathieu Sidokpohou told journalists during a press conference.

"Fortunately the store was closed, so we didn't have any colleague impacted," he said. "So far, business is not the most important thing for us in that region. It's people first."

Many stores in Dubai and other major Middle Eastern shopping hubs shut temporarily or were operating with a skeleton staff as the escalating U.S.-Israel conflict with Iran has roiled the region.

"We have people currently sitting in shelters," Adidas CEO Bjorn Gulden said at the same press conference.

Some 3,000 people work at Adidas' 350 chain and franchise stores in six countries in the region affected by the war.

Gulden said Adidas expected to suffer an impact on revenue from closed stores in Middle East and delays on some products sent via air freight through the region.

(Reporting by Linda Pasquini and Helen Reid; editing by Matthias Williams and Miranda Murray)

data.title: US dollar surge since start of war on Iran unlikely to last, say FX strategists: Reuters poll
data.description:
BENGALURU, March 4 (Reuters) - The U.S. dollar's bounceback since the start of the U.S.-Israel war with Iran may be short-lived due to lingering doubts about the safe-haven appeal of U.S. assets, according to FX strategists polled by Reuters who broadly still expect two Federal Reserve rate cuts later this year.

Traders have been positioned short the dollar - meaning they expect it to fall - since December, with the currency down against a basket of currencies around 12% since the start of 2025. 

The greenback is up about 1.5% since Monday, in large part as short positions were covered, with surging oil prices triggering the move.

JUNE FED CUT NO LONGER PRICED IN

Interest rate futures are no longer pricing a June Fed rate cut, lending some near-term support to the dollar, though contracts are still priced for roughly two cuts by year-end.

Most FX strategists in the monthly Reuters poll, which was almost entirely conducted this week after the first missiles dropped, broadly stuck to calls for the dollar to weaken.

Poll medians from 60 analysts showed the euro up about 2% to $1.18 at end-March. It was then predicted to rise to $1.19 in three months and $1.20 in six - medians broadly unchanged from last month.

"We haven't changed our stance. We're still continuing to expect euro-dollar and various dollar crosses to trade choppily this year," said Jane Foley, head of FX strategy at Rabobank.

"But is the dollar as safe as it used to be? Probably not, because if it was, we wouldn't be having this debate over the last year or so in the first place," she said.

The dollar rally this week has not been a typical "flight to safety" one, given short dollar positioning ahead of the war, which began early on Saturday.

"We had highlighted two weeks ago that some deleveraging signals were appearing in our flows data, perhaps partially linked to U.S.-Iran risk. It was therefore not surprising to see more such deleveraging Monday," JP Morgan FX strategists wrote in a note this week. 

"Were dollar shorts to be more broadly covered back to flat...this would imply support worth +1.5-2% to the dollar from current levels, though much of that depends on the trajectory of the new conflict."

OIL PRICE SPIKE HITS EMERGING CURRENCIES

Asked how positioning would shift by end-March, about half the currency strategists in the survey, 21 of 45, said there would not be much change, or shorts would increase. While 19 said net-shorts would decrease, only five predicted a reversal to net-longs.

While stocks have sold off across the globe this week, traditional safe-havens such as U.S. Treasuries have underperformed and gold, though still up about 20% this year, has slipped.

Brent crude has jumped nearly 15% since Friday on concerns about supply disruptions and is now up around 37% in 2026.

Most emerging market currencies have broadly declined, particularly in Asia, hit by higher oil prices and rising bond yields.

"EM and Latam currencies are suffering from risk-off exacerbation with the double whammy of higher oil and the new jump in real yields... For now, more defensiveness is likely before any attempt of dip-buying," said Alejandro Cuadrado, global head of FX and Latam Strategy at BBVA.

Markets also remain cautious given widespread uncertainty over which U.S. tariffs will eventually apply and when. Concerns about central bank independence have been only partly eased since Kevin Warsh's nomination as the next Fed Chair.

Forecasts a year out are reflecting heightened uncertainty. While the year-ahead median showed the euro strengthening to $1.21, the range of forecasts was about 18 cents, the joint-widest in Reuters polls since October. 

"I'll constantly have these client calls where one person on the team is dollar-bullish, one is dollar-bearish and the other person just decides, 'I'm not even going to look at the dollar'," said Dan Tobon, head of G10 FX at Citi, among the few contrarians who expect the greenback to strengthen.

"This uncertainty on how the U.S. economy and labour market in particular will evolve from here - the range of outcomes - is tremendous. And that uncertainty for now is actually keeping the euro-dollar effectively pegged in a range."

(Other stories from the March foreign exchange poll)

(Reporting by Sarupya Ganguly; Additional Reporting by Gabriel Burin; Polling by Indradip Ghosh and Vijayalakshmi Srinivasan; Editing by Ross Finley and Alex Richardson)

By Sarupya Ganguly

data.title: Fitch Cuts Indonesia's Outlook, Adding to Chorus of Concerns About Policy Uncertainty
data.description:
By Ying Xian Wong


Fitch Ratings cut Indonesia's outlook to negative from stable, adding to the chorus of concerns about policy uncertainty in Southeast Asia's largest economy.

While Fitch expects prudent polices--such as adherence to the fiscal deficit ceiling--to continue, it warned that a stronger push to achieve an ambitious 8% economic growth target and expand social spending could lead to a looser fiscal and monetary stance that may undermine stability.

The move echoes concerns raised by other ratings agencies and index providers, which have flagged a lack of transparency and predictability in policymaking as a risk for markets and the broader economy.

Indonesian assets have experienced bouts of volatility as a result, with stocks and bonds selling off and the rupiah weakening as investors pulled money from the market.

Recent decisions by index providers FTSE Russell and MSCI to halt reviews of Indonesian securities over transparency issues have fanned long-held concerns about the credibility of policymaking.

Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto has defended the government's expansive social programs, saying that they will boost productivity, advance the economy and save costs in the long run.

Market regulators have also taken action, moving swiftly to address concerns raised by the index providers with reform proposals.

In a report last month, S&P Global Ratings said it believes authorities are working to address issues that have led to foreign portfolio outflows, viewing the damage to investor confidence as reversible.

S&P maintained a stable outlook on Indonesia, saying recent market volatility has had limited impact on sovereign credit metrics for now. However, it warned that a failure to restore investor confidence could raise financing costs and hurt investment.

"Further deterioration stemming from the stock market volatility could raise the likelihood of a negative rating action," said S&P analyst Kim Eng Tan.

Both Fitch and Moody's Ratings have now downgraded Indonesia's credit outlook to negative from stable while affirming their respective ratings on the country's debt.

Fitch said its affirmation reflects Indonesia's track record of macroeconomic and financial stability, solid growth prospects and a relatively modest government debt burden.

However, those strengths are constrained by weak revenue collection, high debt-servicing costs and governance indicators that lag behind those of its peers, it added.

"Indonesia's policy framework credibility and consistency are being tested," Fitch said.


Write to Ying Xian Wong at yingxian.wong@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

03-04-26 0557ET

data.title: Fed's Hammack backs steady interest rates, tells NYT it's too early to gauge Iran war impact
TSX futures rise as Middle East crisis boosts oil, gold prices
data.description:
March 4 (Reuters) - Futures tracking Canada's main stock index rose on Wednesday as rising tensions in the Middle East pumped up oil prices for the fourth straight day, while safe-haven demand boosted gold prices. 

March futures on the S&P/TSX composite index were up 0.36%, as of 5:20 a.m. ET.

The conflict has jolted investors worldwide as it heads into its fifth day. Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon said it would take a couple of weeks for the markets to digest the impact of the war on Iran.

Oil prices gained but at a slower pace than in previous sessions as traders weighed concerns of supply disruption from the Middle East.

President Donald Trump said on Tuesday the U.S. Navy could begin escorting tankers through the Strait of Hormuz if necessary.

Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said on Wednesday the ongoing conflict was a "failure" of the international order and the U.S. had not consulted its allies before striking Iran.

Brent crude futures gained 3% and U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude was up 2.8%. 

Spot gold gained more than 2%, recovering from the previous session's drop, as investors rushed for the safe-haven asset. Silver rose 5.4% and copper prices also firmed. 

Toronto's benchmark index had slipped 2.2% on Tuesday, logging its biggest decline in nearly three weeks, as fears of inflation over the extended Middle East crisis resulted in a broad-based decline led by miners.

In corporate news, a Brazilian court halted the transfer of mineral rights to a gold asset sale from Equinox Gold to Chinese metal miner CMOC on Tuesday. 

FOR CANADIAN MARKETS NEWS, CLICK ON CODES:

TSX market report [.TO]

Canadian dollar and bonds report [CAD/] [CA/]

Reuters global stocks poll for Canada

Canadian markets directory

(Reporting by Utkarsh Tushar Hathi; Editing by Shreya Biswas)

data.title: Adidas disappoints with its 2026 outlook, shares fall back to 3-year lows
data.description:
Targets deemed too timid by analysts

On the occasion of the presentation of its final 2025 full-year results, the group said it expects sales growth of between 5% and 9% in 2026 at constant exchange rates, a target that appears broadly in line with the consensus, which had been looking for a 7% increase
.
Its operating profit, however, is expected to come in at around €2.3bn this year, up from the €2.06bn posted in 2025, but well below the consensus estimate of €2.7bn.

Adidas says it wants to continue gaining market share over the medium term, leading it to forecast average annual revenue growth at constant exchange rates of 5% to 9% through 2028, with operating profit expected to rise by around 15% a year over the period.

Here again, those projections are proving disappointing, given that the market had been hoping for mid-term annual operating-profit growth of around 19%.

"The whole question is how conservative this guidance is, given Adidas' habit of playing it safe at the start of the year," RBC analysts noted this morning.

A nonetheless promising end to 2025

At Jefferies, analysts lament that these targets push into the background the sportswear maker's strong finish to 2025. That resulted in 11% growth at constant exchange rates at the brand level in the fourth quarter, delivering net revenue of €6.1bn and an operating margin up 0.1 percentage point to 50.8%.

As a result, operating profit more than doubled to €164m over the final three months of the year.

More optimistic investors will focus on the announcement of a 40% increase in the annual dividend to €2.80 a share and the launch of a new share buyback program of up to €1.5bn.

The renewal of chief executive Bjorn Gulden's mandate, widely appreciated by the market, for an additional three years (that is, through 2030) was also welcomed by analysts, as was the appointment as chairman of the board of Egyptian businessman Nassef Sawiris, a "heavyweight" in the business world in RBC's words.

A sportswear sector still under pressure

 That news, however, was not enough to dispel the sense of unease that has surrounded the footwear, sporting-goods and sportswear fashion sector for several months. The climate is well illustrated by the recent guidance issued by rival Puma, which expects its revenue to fall this year despite the staging of the Football World Cup, an event that typically tends to boost sales.

Nike, meanwhile, is set to publish its quarterly results on Thursday, March 31.

data.title: Online travel agent Loveholidays delays IPO on MidEast war - FT
data.description:
(Alliance News) - Loveholidays, an online travel agent that was expected to be the first major listing on the London Stock Exchange in 2026, is considering a delay to its initial public offering because of the travel chaos caused by the war in the Middle East, the Financial Times reported on Tuesday.

Loveholidays had been expected to announce plans to float in early March, aiming for a valuation of GBP1 billion, according to the FT.

However, it is now considering a delay, the newspaper said, citing "people familiar with the matter". A new date could be after the Easter holiday, one of the people said.

Easter falls on April 5 this year, and the four-day holiday weekend is normally a heavy travel period.

Loveholidays is being advised by Rothschild, Barclays, JPMorgan and Investec, the FT said.

By Tom Waite, Alliance News editor

Comments and questions to newsroom@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2026 Alliance News Ltd. All Rights Reserved.

ASMPT Limited Reports Earnings Results for the Full Year Ended December 31, 2025
data.description:
ASMPT Limited reported earnings results for the full year ended December 31, 2025. For the full year, the company reported sales was HKD 13,736.24 million compared to HKD 12,483.23 million a year ago. Net income was HKD 902.03 million compared to HKD 345.26 million a year ago.

Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was HKD 2.61 compared to HKD 0.71 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was HKD 2.6 compared to HKD 0.71 a year ago. Basic earnings per share was HKD 2.17 compared to HKD 0.83 a year ago.

Diluted earnings per share was HKD 2.16 compared to HKD 0.83 a year ago.
